title: lolium temulentum f. asperum
intro: Lolium temulentum f. asperum, also known as darnel or cheat grass, is a wild grass belonging to the family Poaceae, characterized by its tall, annual growth and distinctive awned spikelets. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat digestive issues, such as nausea and indigestion, due to its bitter and astringent properties. The health benefits of Lolium temulentum f. asperum include its potential to aid in weight loss, improve liver function, and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used as a tea, infusion, or tincture to leverage its medicinal properties.
